name: Yue Fei
description: Yue Fei (1103-1142) was a renowned Chinese military general who lived during the Southern Song Dynasty. He is celebrated as a national hero for his unwavering loyalty and his valiant efforts in defending the Song Dynasty from the invading Jurchen Jin Dynasty forces. Born into a peasant family, Yue Fei displayed exceptional martial skills and strategic brilliance from a young age. He rose through the ranks of the Song army, leading his troops to numerous victories against the Jin invaders. His most famous battles include the defense of Yancheng and Yingchang, where his outnumbered forces decisively defeated the Jin armies. Yue Fei's unwavering patriotism and his commitment to reclaiming lost territories earned him immense popularity among the people. However, his success and popularity aroused the jealousy and suspicion of the corrupt court officials, particularly the Grand Councilor Qin Hui. Qin Hui, influenced by the Jin Dynasty, framed Yue Fei on false charges of treason and had him executed in 1142. Despite his tragic death, Yue Fei's legacy endures as a symbol of patriotism, loyalty, and military prowess in Chinese culture. He is often depicted as a righteous and incorruptible figure, and his story serves as an inspiration for generations of Chinese people. Yue Fei's spirit of unwavering loyalty and dedication to his country continues to resonate in Chinese society today, making him one of the most revered figures in Chinese history.
subtitle: Patriotic General of the Song Dynasty
achievements: Defended the Song Dynasty from Jurchen invasions
Won numerous battles against the Jin army
Inspired patriotic fervor among the people
Became a symbol of loyalty and righteousness
His story continues to inspire generations

keyMyths.title: Yue Fei's Tattoo
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei's mother tattooed the words 'Serve the country with utmost loyalty' on his back to remind him of his duty. This act symbolized his unwavering dedication to his country and became a defining aspect of his legend.

keyMyths.title: The Battle of Yancheng
keyMyths.description: Despite being outnumbered, Yue Fei's army decisively defeated the Jin forces at Yancheng using innovative tactics and unwavering courage. This victory significantly boosted the morale of the Song Dynasty and solidified Yue Fei's reputation as a brilliant strategist.
keyMyths.source: Historical Records of the Song Dynasty

keyMyths.title: The Tragic Execution
keyMyths.description: Yue Fei was falsely accused of treason and executed on the orders of Qin Hui, a corrupt official who colluded with the Jin Dynasty. His unjust death became a symbol of political corruption and betrayal, further solidifying his status as a national hero.
keyMyths.source: Historical Records of the Song Dynasty

sources.author: James T.C. Liu
sources.title: Yueh Fei and China's Heritage of Loyalty
sources.description: A detailed biography of Yue Fei that examines his life, military career, and political context.

sources.author: Helmut Wilhelm
sources.title: The Image of Yueh Fei
sources.description: An analysis of Yue Fei's evolving image in Chinese culture and the various interpretations of his story.

associations.name: Loyalty
associations.significance: Unwavering devotion to the Song Dynasty and his emperor, even in the face of adversity and betrayal.

associations.name: Filial Piety
associations.significance: Deep respect and care for his mother, famously exemplified by the tattoo she inscribed on his back.

associations.name: Spear
associations.significance: His primary weapon, symbolizing his martial prowess and military leadership.

associations.name: Crane
associations.significance: Associated with longevity and righteousness, reflecting Yue Fei's enduring legacy and moral character.

associations.name: Patriotism
associations.significance: His fierce love for his country and his willingness to sacrifice everything to defend it.

associations.name: The Tattoo 'Serve the Country with Utmost Loyalty'
associations.significance: Represents his unwavering commitment and the burden of responsibility he carried.

associations.name: Battlefield
associations.significance: The arena where Yue Fei demonstrated his military genius and defended the Song Dynasty.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: A citizen-soldier who left his farm to lead the army and defend Rome, then voluntarily relinquished power.
A national heroine who led the French army to victory in the Hundred Years' War, driven by faith and patriotism.
A loyal samurai who fought to protect Emperor Go-Daigo during the Genkō War, embodying unwavering loyalty and sacrifice.
A legendary leader who united the Britons and defended them against invaders, symbolizing justice and chivalry.

worshipPractices.name: Yue Fei Temple Visits
worshipPractices.description: Many temples dedicated to Yue Fei exist throughout China. Worshippers visit these temples to pay their respects, offer prayers for protection and success, and learn about his life and virtues. The most famous is the Yue Fei Temple in Hangzhou, near his tomb.

worshipPractices.name: Storytelling and Opera
worshipPractices.description: Yue Fei's story is frequently depicted in Chinese opera and storytelling traditions. These performances emphasize his loyalty, bravery, and tragic fate, reinforcing his status as a national hero and moral exemplar. They ensure the continuation of his legend through generations.

cultural.modernLegacy: Yue Fei remains a powerful symbol of patriotism, loyalty, and righteousness in Chinese culture. He is often invoked as an example of moral courage and selfless service to the nation. His story continues to inspire people in China and around the world.

extendedContent.title: Early Life and Training
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei was born in 1103 in Xiangzhou (modern Tangyin County, Henan province) during the Northern Song Dynasty. Coming from humble peasant origins, he was orphaned at a young age. Despite his circumstances, Yue Fei displayed exceptional strength, intelligence, and martial aptitude. He was taught by Zhou Tong, a renowned military instructor, mastering various weapons and combat techniques, especially the spear. Legend states that his mother tattooed the words "Serve the Country with Utmost Loyalty" (精忠報國) on his back to remind him of his duty. This act became a powerful symbol of filial piety and patriotism. He passed the military examination at a young age and formally joined the Song army, quickly distinguishing himself through courage and tactical acumen during battles against bandits and local rebellions. His early experiences forged his resolve and prepared him for the larger conflicts to come.

extendedContent.title: Greatest Quests and Battles
extendedContent.content: Yue Fei's most significant contributions came during the Jurchen Jin Dynasty's invasion of the Song Dynasty. He consistently achieved remarkable victories against numerically superior Jin forces, becoming a key figure in the Southern Song's defense. His strategies were innovative, emphasizing discipline, mobility, and exploiting enemy weaknesses. The Battle of Yancheng is particularly famous, where Yue Fei's outnumbered troops decisively defeated the Jin army. He recovered vast territories previously occupied by the Jin, earning the respect of his soldiers and the admiration of the Song populace. Yue Fei instilled a strong sense of discipline and loyalty within his army, known as the "Yue Family Army", making them a formidable fighting force. His campaigns aimed to recapture the former Song capital of Kaifeng and restore the dynasty's lost territories, but these ambitions were ultimately thwarted by political machinations.

extendedContent.title: Tragic End and Enduring Legacy
extendedContent.content: Despite his military successes, Yue Fei fell victim to court intrigue. The Song Chancellor, Qin Hui, advocated for a peace treaty with the Jin Dynasty, fearing Yue Fei's growing power and popularity. Qin Hui, along with his wife Lady Wang and other accomplices, framed Yue Fei on false charges of treason. In 1142, Yue Fei was executed in prison, along with his son Yue Yun and General Zhang Xian. His death was widely mourned by the Song people, who recognized his innocence and patriotism. Years later, Yue Fei was posthumously exonerated and honored as a national hero. His story became a symbol of loyalty, integrity, and the dangers of political corruption. Temples were erected in his honor, and his spirit was invoked as a guardian against invaders. Yue Fei's legacy continues to inspire generations of Chinese people, serving as a reminder of the importance of defending one's country and upholding moral principles.

shortDescription: Yue Fei (1103-1142) was a Chinese military general and national hero, famed for his unwavering loyalty and defense of the Song Dynasty against the Jurchen Jin forces.
